title: Wasm baseline truthfulness — duplicate-free VM construction and an executed Node test gate
purpose: The executable baseline governed by `docs/design/beamr/WASM-PORT-ARC.md` at `5b165cd`, specifically WPORT-1 (`docs/design/beamr/WASM-PORT-ARC.md:46-57`). At the pinned product base `d9de35e`, `WasmVm::new` returns on BIF-registration error (`crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:59-70`), and its caller registers gate 1 followed by gate 2, exception, ETF, and stdlib stubs (`crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:779-788`) even though gate 1 already includes exception and ETF registration (`crates/beamr/src/native/bifs.rs:47-65`); the registry refuses duplicate MFAs (`crates/beamr/src/native/mod.rs:251-268`). The evidence pack's executed wasm runner recorded three conversion tests passing and both VM actor tests failing at their constructor calls with the duplicate-registration error (`_inputs/wasm-port-evidence-pack-full.md:52-68`; `crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:916-918`; `crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:971-973`). CI did not expose that runtime refusal because the current workflow installs the target and runs only two `cargo check` commands (`.github/workflows/cooperative-wasm.yml:13-20`). Compiles does not imply constructs, and constructs does not imply runs: this brief buys the first truthful executable fact and makes CI prove it permanently, without entering WPORT-2 scheduling scope.
task: Correct the wasm-safe BIF caller set so it composes gate 1, gate 2, and stdlib stubs exactly once while preserving the registry's duplicate-MFA refusal as a hard wall. Make the complete existing five-test `#[wasm_bindgen_test]` inventory pass under real `wasm32-unknown-unknown` execution with the Node wasm-bindgen runner, including both actor workloads after construction. Extend `.github/workflows/cooperative-wasm.yml` without removing either existing check so CI installs the matching wasm test toolchain, executes that suite, reports what commit/toolchain and test count it measured, and carries fail-first evidence that the new leg detects the pre-fix constructor defect. Deviations from this brief — including improvements — STOP and report; they are not improvised (house rule, composition-campaign precedent).

requirements.title: Correct the caller's registration set; preserve duplicate refusal as the wall
requirements.spec: At `d9de35e`, `register_wasm_safe_bifs` calls `register_gate1_bifs`, `register_gate2_bifs`, `register_exception_bifs`, `register_etf_bifs`, and `register_stdlib_stubs` in that order (`crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:779-788`), while gate 1 already calls the exception and ETF registrars (`crates/beamr/src/native/bifs.rs:47-65`). The exact duplicated MFA set is seven entries: `erlang:raise/3` (`crates/beamr/src/native/exception_bifs.rs:11-22`) plus `erlang:term_to_binary/1`, `erlang:term_to_binary/2`, `erlang:term_to_iovec/1`, `erlang:term_to_iovec/2`, `erlang:binary_to_term/1`, and `erlang:binary_to_term/2` (`crates/beamr/src/native/etf_bifs.rs:15-31`). The source-byte table comparison is complete: gate 2 has no MFA overlap with expanded gate 1, stdlib stubs have no MFA overlap with expanded gate 1, and gate 2 and stdlib stubs have no overlap with each other (`crates/beamr/src/native/bifs.rs:23-82`; `crates/beamr/src/native/code_management_bifs.rs:42-67`; `crates/beamr/src/native/dictionary_bifs.rs:14-38`; `crates/beamr/src/native/etf_bifs.rs:15-31`; `crates/beamr/src/native/ets_bifs.rs:107-193`; `crates/beamr/src/native/exception_bifs.rs:11-22`; `crates/beamr/src/native/process_info_bifs.rs:30-45`; `crates/beamr/src/native/process_info_bifs.rs:147-156`; `crates/beamr/src/native/system_info_bifs.rs:20-28`; `crates/beamr/src/native/system_info_bifs.rs:101-110`; `crates/beamr/src/native/process_bifs/mod.rs:21-56`; `crates/beamr/src/native/stdlib_stubs/registrations.rs:73-658`; `crates/beamr/src/native/stdlib_stubs/registrations.rs:661-697`). Therefore fix only the caller: remove the two redundant explicit exception/ETF registrations so the wasm-safe set is gate 1 + gate 2 + stdlib stubs. Do not change any registrar table or BIF implementation. Do not weaken `BifRegistryImpl::register_entry`: an occupied `(module, function, arity)` must continue to return `NativeRegistrationError::DuplicateMfa` (`crates/beamr/src/native/mod.rs:251-268`); no idempotent-registration escape hatch, ignore-duplicates flag, or caught-and-discarded duplicate error is permitted.
acceptance: `WasmVm::new()` succeeds under the wasm32 runner, and the exported `create_vm()` path, which delegates to `WasmVm::new` at `crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:38-61`, also returns a constructed real VM

The final `register_wasm_safe_bifs` caller contains exactly gate 1 + gate 2 + stdlib stubs; the explicit exception and ETF calls are absent, and no registrar table or registered native function/capability/dirty-kind entry changes

Duplicate refusal remains a loud construction wall: `BifRegistryImpl::register_entry` still returns `DuplicateMfa` for an occupied MFA, and fail-first verification by deliberately reintroducing one removed duplicate makes construction fail rather than silently succeeding
No behavior change to any registered BIF; no new `#[allow]`; no new `unwrap`, `expect`, or panic outside tests

requirements.title: The complete existing wasm-bindgen suite passes under real Node wasm execution
requirements.spec: The wrapper already declares `wasm-bindgen-test` as a dev dependency (`crates/beamr-wasm/Cargo.toml:28-29`) and contains exactly five `#[wasm_bindgen_test]` cases at `d9de35e`: `converts_complex_nested_js_object_to_term` (`crates/beamr-wasm/src/convert.rs:390-440`), `converts_terms_to_js_values` (`crates/beamr-wasm/src/convert.rs:442-490`), `documents_boolean_atom_round_trip_as_atom_names` (`crates/beamr-wasm/src/convert.rs:492-501`), `await_vm_call_resolves_with_js_handler_reply` (`crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:916-959`), and `pump_once_drives_an_actor_call_to_completion` (`crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:971-1021`). The evidence pack records that the Node-capable runner is executable at this base: the three conversion tests pass, while both actor tests stop at `WasmVm::new().expect("VM constructs")` before their workloads (`_inputs/wasm-port-evidence-pack-full.md:52-68`; `crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:918`; `crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:973`). Run the full existing suite with `CARGO_TARGET_WASM32_UNKNOWN_UNKNOWN_RUNNER=wasm-bindgen-test-runner cargo test --manifest-path crates/beamr-wasm/Cargo.toml --target wasm32-unknown-unknown --locked -- --nocapture`; after R1, both actor tests must pass their real JS-handler/Promise workloads, not merely construction. Preserve all five tests and their real VM path. NO-POLLING binds the test work: this brief may not introduce a poll, retry, sleep, recurring timer, animation-frame loop, or bounded readiness loop to wait for wasm/runner readiness; construction is direct and asynchronous completion is event/Promise-driven. Existing workload-driving behavior is not scheduling redesign and may not be expanded under this brief.
acceptance: The Node wasm-bindgen runner reports all five named tests passing on `wasm32-unknown-unknown`: three conversion tests and two VM actor tests

`await_vm_call_resolves_with_js_handler_reply` reaches the actor workload and resolves the real `CallFuture` Promise with result `42`; `pump_once_drives_an_actor_call_to_completion` reaches its workload and resolves with result `8` (`crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:908-959`; `crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:961-1021`)
Zero wasm-bindgen tests are skipped, ignored, disabled, renamed out of discovery, converted to expected failures, or replaced by a JavaScript VM stub
No new poll/retry/readiness loop is introduced; wasm readiness is proved by direct construction and completion remains event/Promise-driven

requirements.title: CI executes the wasm suite as a freshness-stamped truthfulness gate
requirements.spec: The current workflow has one `wasm32-unknown-unknown` job: checkout, target installation, `cargo check -p beamr --no-default-features --features cooperative,json --locked`, and `cargo check --manifest-path crates/beamr-wasm/Cargo.toml --target wasm32-unknown-unknown --locked` (`.github/workflows/cooperative-wasm.yml:10-20`). Keep both checks unchanged in intent and add an executed-test leg that installs a `wasm-bindgen-test-runner` compatible with the lockfile, confirms Node is available, and runs R2's exact locked cargo-test command. The gate must print the measured commit and toolchain identity (`git rev-parse HEAD`, Rust/Cargo, wasm-bindgen runner, and Node versions) before execution, and its log must expose the five discovered test names plus an explicit five-test pass count rather than treating an unannotated exit zero as sufficient. Record fail-first evidence in the handoff, not as a committed defect: running the new CI test command against pre-R1 `d9de35e` must fail at the two constructor sites with the duplicate-registration error recorded by the evidence pack (`_inputs/wasm-port-evidence-pack-full.md:52-68`); the same command on the brief's final head must pass. Believed-state-is-not-citable-state binds this gate: the handoff and CI log state exactly which commit, target, toolchain, runner, Node version, command, discovered tests, and result were measured. NO-POLLING also binds the CI/test addition: no readiness polling may be added to make the runner appear green.
acceptance: Both pre-existing wasm32 `cargo check` commands remain in `.github/workflows/cooperative-wasm.yml` and pass on the final head
CI installs the matching wasm-bindgen test runner and executes the locked `beamr-wasm` suite on `wasm32-unknown-unknown` under Node; it does not stop at compilation or type-check

Fail-first proof is recorded in the handoff: the new command at `d9de35e` fails with `native function already registered for Atom(76):Atom(125)/3` at the two VM constructor tests; the identical command on the final head passes
The CI log prints commit and toolchain freshness data and names all five tests with an explicit count of five passed, zero failed, zero ignored/skipped
The CI/test diff introduces no JavaScript VM stub and no poll/retry/readiness loop

boundaries: No scheduling redesign, wake/pump/timer change, run-to-idle change, rAF retirement, or generated `runUntilExit` change — those are WPORT-2/WPORT-3 scope
No new BIFs and no registration-semantics change beyond correcting the caller's set; registrar tables and `DuplicateMfa` refusal remain unchanged

No threaded-surface or feature-closure change: `beamr-wasm` remains on the cooperative,json closure shown at `crates/beamr-wasm/Cargo.toml:13-21`, without `threads`, `net`, `fs`, `jit`, `embedded`, or readiness work
No JavaScript-stub substitution anywhere: construction and both actor workloads use the real Rust/wasm VM

Believed state is not citable state binds every current-state assertion in this brief: facts are pinned to file:line at `d9de35e` or to the recorded executed evidence; prospective shapes are requirements, not claims (`docs/design/beamr/WASM-PORT-ARC.md:25-29` at `5b165cd`)

NO-POLLING binds the test and CI work even though scheduling is out of scope: no new test may introduce a poll/retry/sleep/rAF/recurring-timer loop to await wasm readiness; use direct construction and event/Promise completion only (`docs/design/beamr/WASM-PORT-ARC.md:17-23` at `5b165cd`)
verification: Run the house bar on the final head: `cargo fmt --check`; fresh `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings`; fresh `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings`; `cargo test -p beamr --lib`; `cargo test -p beamr --test '*'`; and 10 consecutive isolated runs of every new or changed acceptance pin. Run both existing wasm checks from `.github/workflows/cooperative-wasm.yml:17-20`, then run `CARGO_TARGET_WASM32_UNKNOWN_UNKNOWN_RUNNER=wasm-bindgen-test-runner cargo test --manifest-path crates/beamr-wasm/Cargo.toml --target wasm32-unknown-unknown --locked -- --nocapture` and require the five named wasm-bindgen tests to report five passed, zero failed, zero ignored/skipped. Stamp the handoff with final `git rev-parse HEAD`, `rustc --version --verbose`, `cargo --version`, `wasm-bindgen-test-runner --version`, `node --version`, target, exact commands, and timestamps so the measured state is citable. Record R3 fail-first evidence from the same wasm test command on `d9de35e`, where the two VM tests fail at `crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:918` and `crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:973` with the duplicate-registration error; do not commit the fail-first mutation. Grep the diff to prove zero new `#[allow]`, and inspect the diff to prove no new non-test `unwrap`/`expect`/panic, no registrar/registry-semantics change, no JS stub, and no readiness-polling loop.
